raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I rented a car from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. My prepaid reservation totaled {$210.00}, and all mandatory fees were disclosed at the time of booking. At pickup, the merchant added a new charge labeled Handling Insurance Fee Plus, which was never disclosed during the reservation process. This fee was {$7.00} per day plus 16.5 % tax and was described as mandatory if I declined their XXXX coverage. This is not a normal insurance product or a service ; it is a penalty for declining coverage. 

I arrived after a long shuttle delay and had no practical alternative at that hour. The merchant refused to release the vehicle unless I accepted the undisclosed fee. This resulted in a baitandswitch situation where the actual required price was significantly higher than the reservation price. 

I disputed the charge with my credit card company. The bank denied the dispute solely because I signed the rental agreement at pickup. This does not address the core issue : the fee was not disclosed at the time of reservation and was not optional. I attempted to reopen the dispute with a detailed written explanation, but the bank again refused to reconsider the case and their online system now blocks me from submitting a corrected dispute basis, stating that the transaction has already been disputed. 

I requested information from both the merchant and the bank, including documentation showing where this fee was disclosed during booking. Neither party provided this information. I am asking the CFPB to require the bank to reopen the dispute under the correct basis ( misrepresentation / undisclosed mandatory fee ) and to investigate whether the merchant failed to honor the reservation terms.
